summary: bin/rails test (RAILS_ENV=test, run serially) executes 234 runs / 766 assertions with 0 failures but 3 errors, all in test/services/phases/restart_define_test.rb. Each errors with ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique on index_step_runs_unique_without_shard because the test creates a new step_runs row with iteration: 1 (attempt defaulting to 1) for steps(:requirements_writer), colliding with the step_runs(:requirements_ready) fixture which already occupies (step_id, iteration=1, attempt=1) for that same step. bin/rubocop is clean (151 files, no offenses). Note: the default parallel test run (bin/rails test with fork-based parallelize(workers: :number_of_processors)) hung indefinitely with zombie child processes in this sandboxed environment; re-running with PARALLEL_WORKERS=1 completed normally and surfaced the real failures below, so this is an environment quirk unrelated to the app code.

findings.issue: Three tests ("refuses to restart while a step is active", "does not carry non-human feedback", "carries prior human-tagged feedback onto the seeded run") each call `@requirements.step_runs.create!(state: ..., iteration: 1, required_role: "requirements", ...)` without setting `attempt`. Since `attempt` defaults to 1 and the setup block already leaves step_runs(:requirements_ready) at (step: requirements_writer, iteration: 1, attempt: 1, now state: succeeded), the new row collides with that fixture and raises ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique on index_step_runs_unique_without_shard (DETAIL: Key (step_id, iteration, attempt)=(465594245, 1, 1) already exists). Reproduce with: PARALLEL_WORKERS=1 RAILS_ENV=test bin/rails test test/services/phases/restart_define_test.rb.
